{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Nimzowitsch Defence| |rd| |bd|qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|= |pd|pd|pd| |pd|pd|pd|pd|= | | |nd| | | | | |= | | | |pd| | | | |= | | | |pl|pl| | | |= | | |nl| | | | | |= |pl|pl|pl| | |pl|pl|pl|= |rl| |bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= || }} = Nimzowitsch Defence = Black now faces a strong gambit that if accepted allows white to gain time by attacking the queen's knight. There are several options for black: :[[/3... dxe4/]] accepts the gambit. White should play d5 to attack the knight. :[[/3... e6/]] transposes into an inferior variation of the [[wikipedia:French Defence|French Defence]] with the knight blocking the c-pawn. :[[/3... a6/]] an interesting way to decline the gambit as it ignores the attack on d5. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}. '1. e4 Nc6 2. d4 d5 3. Nc3'
